channel.title: Research on B3N.B4UR_
channel.description: Recent content in Research on B3N.B4UR_

item.title: Leading Without Authority — What 15 Years Taught Me About Influence
item.description:
Leading Without Authority — What 15 Years Taught Me About Influence

The Meeting That Changed Everything

It was early 2022, and I was sitting in a virtual meeting room with 14 people across four countries. I’d just been given responsibility for a team of Advanced Cloud Experts spanning EMEA — architects in Germany, the UK, the Netherlands, France. Senior, experienced, brilliant people. Most of them had been doing this work longer than I’d been at Microsoft.

item.title: Why 80% of AI Projects Fail — And What Leaders Can Do About It
item.description:
TL;DR

RAND Corporation researchers interviewed 65 data scientists and engineers to find out why AI projects fail at twice the rate of other IT projects. The answer isn’t better algorithms — it’s better leadership. Five root causes explain the overwhelming majority of failures, and every single one is preventable. This post breaks down the research, maps it to what I see in the field, and gives you a practical playbook to beat the odds.

item.title: Windows 365 for AI Agents — Why Cloud PCs Are the Enterprise Runtime for Agentic AI
item.description:
TL;DR

What Detail
The problem Most enterprise automation stalls at UI-driven legacy apps where APIs are incomplete or don’t exist
What’s new Windows 365 for Agents gives AI agents their own Cloud PCs — full desktop sessions with enterprise identity, Intune management, and policy enforcement
How it works Agent 365 governs what agents can do; Windows 365 for Agents defines where they execute — inside a managed, auditable Cloud PC
Why it matters Same Entra ID, Conditional Access, and compliance controls you apply to human users now apply to agent sessions
Who should care Enterprise architects, IT admins, and automation teams running UI-dependent workflows at scale

Why This Matters

Here’s an uncomfortable truth about enterprise automation: the most critical business workflows still live behind UIs that nobody ever built APIs for.

item.title: Intune & Windows 365 – Naming Conventions & Best Practices Cheatsheet
item.description: If you’ve ever inherited an Intune tenant where policies are named Test Policy 2 - Copy (final_v3) — you know the pain. Naming conventions aren’t glamorous, but they’re the foundation of a manageable, scalable endpoint environment. This post covers battle-tested naming patterns for Microsoft Intune and Windows 365, distilled from Microsoft documentation, MVP community guidance, and my own field experience deploying Cloud PCs at scale.

item.title: Business Envisioning Workshop — The Preparation Questionnaire I Send Every Customer
item.description:
Why This Exists

Here’s a pattern I see in almost every customer engagement: the workshop is booked, the room is full, the whiteboard is ready — and the first 90 minutes get burned on context-setting because nobody aligned on the problem beforehand.

item.title: First 60 Days
item.description:
From AI Excitement to Real Impact: Reflections from the Microsoft Innovation Hub

A Moment of Gratitude

Before I dive into the reflections from these first 60 days, I want to pause and acknowledge something I don’t take for granted.

item.title: Chief of Staff Agents — The Story Behind B3N.B4UR_
item.description:
Chief of Staff Agents

The AI team behind B3N.B4UR_

This is the story of how a Technical Architect at Microsoft asked an AI to help fix a few typos — and ended up with a full content operations system, a brand identity, a publishing strategy, and an agent that refers to him as “Chief.”

item.title: How AI Became My Personal Chief of Staff Team
item.description:
How AI Became My Personal Chief of Staff Team

I’ve spent years helping enterprises adopt AI. I’ve run workshops, scored use cases with the BXT Framework, and told countless customers: “Start with a real problem, not a technology.”

item.title: Azure Default Outbound Access Retirement — What It Means for AVD & Windows 365 and What You Should Do Now
item.description:
TL;DR

On March 31, 2026, newly created Azure Virtual Networks will default to private subnets — meaning no implicit outbound internet access. If you run Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) or Windows 365 with Azure Network Connections (ANC), your session hosts require outbound connectivity to function. Without explicit outbound access, provisioning will fail and existing session hosts on new VNets will lose connectivity to critical services like Windows activation, agent updates, and RDP brokering.

item.title: Cloud Adoption Framework vs. AI Adoption Framework — A Side-by-Side Comparison
item.description: If you’ve spent any time in Azure-land, you know the Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF). It’s the playbook — seven phases that walk an organisation from “why cloud?” all the way to “how do I keep this thing running?” I’ve used it in dozens of engagements, and it remains one of the most practical artefacts Microsoft has ever published.

item.title: How a Frontier Center of Excellence Works Together with a Cloud Center of Excellence
item.description: As a Technical Architect in Microsoft’s Innovation Hub and Office of the CTO, I sit right at the intersection where cloud platform strategy meets frontier AI ambition. I spend my days working with customers who’ve already invested years building mature Cloud Centers of Excellence — and who are now asking the same question: “We’ve got the cloud part figured out. How do we bolt on an AI capability without breaking what works?”

item.title: The BXT Framework Cheatsheet — Business, Experience, Technology
item.description: Welcome to the boardroom! Every week I sit in rooms — physical and virtual — where customers pitch AI use cases. Some are brilliant. Some are solutions looking for a problem. The hardest part of my job isn’t building the thing — it’s making sure we’re building the right thing.

item.title: Ben's Cosio
item.description:
Ben’s Cosio — Innovation Hub Agent

One agent. Three skills. Zero guessing.

Ben’s Cosio is the single front door for Microsoft Architects preparing, running, and following up on customer engagements. It replaces the need for multiple standalone agents with one intelligent assistant that applies the right framework when it matters.

item.title: About
item.description:
Ben Martin Baur

Technical Architect · Office of the CTO · Microsoft Innovation Hub Germany

👀 About Me

Hi, I’m Ben — a Technical Architect working in the Office of the CTO at the Microsoft Innovation Hub for Germany and Austria. With over 15 years of international experience in enterprise architecture, End-User Computing, and cloud technologies, I help customers to turn their vision into scalable architectures. Within the Innovation Hub, I operate at the intersection of technology strategy, AI transformation and customer impact.

item.title: Writing
item.description:
Writing

I’ve never been more excited about technology. I am not a developer, never been an expert in writing powershell or any other type of script. For the first time in my career, anything I want to build, write or create in the software world feels fundamentally within my grasp.

item.title: Let's get Real or Let's Not Play - Checklist
item.description:
Let’s Get Real or Let’s Not Play – a Consultant Checklist

Based on Transforming the Buyer/Seller Relationship”* by Mahan Khalsa and Randy Illig*

item.title: Welcome to the boardroom
item.description:
Welcome to the Board Room: AI as a C-Level Imperative

TL;DR

Role Priority
CEO Drives AI-powered transformation for innovation, growth, culture, risk, and talent
CIO Aligns tech and business strategy; modernises IT with AI & data for agility and value
CTO Leads adoption of cutting-edge technologies; ensures robust architecture & responsible AI governance
CISO Protects data & compliance; mitigates AI-driven security risks and regulatory challenges
CFO Focuses on cost optimisation, ROI, and risk management for AI investments
CHRO Enhances workforce productivity & skills with AI; fosters adaptive culture and change

The Shift in the Boardroom

AI has moved from the IT department to the boardroom. Once a topic of experimentation, it is now a strategic imperative. CEOs are placing AI at the heart of their growth and innovation agendas, with nearly 75% claiming to be the primary decision-maker on AI. CIOs are becoming strategy architects, aligning AI with business outcomes.

item.title: Markdown Syntax Guide
item.description: This article provides a comprehensive reference for Markdown syntax that can be used in Hugo content files. Use this as a quick reference when writing blog posts.

item.title: Vegan my story
item.description:
VEGAN – MY STORY

When I first embraced veganism with my wife, it wasn’t because someone told me I should. It wasn’t because of a trend or a fleeting guilt after watching a documentary. We were in Australia and out of a sudden we noticed that there were hundreds of plant-based options, even in the most remote location, there was a plant-based option. At the same time it was fairly hard to travel with fresh animal products in a campervan. So we thought, let’s try this!

item.title: Rich Content & Shortcodes Guide
item.description: This guide covers the various shortcodes and rich content features available in the Hugo Stack theme. Shortcodes extend Markdown with powerful embedding and formatting capabilities.
